title: Digital-twin data contract
description: Payload crossing the device-communication seam between a digital twin and a control system. Structural validity only — satisfying this schema means a non-learned gate can evaluate the proposal, never that the proposal is safe or authorized.

asset_id.description: which physical asset the payload speaks for

correlation_id.description: ties proposal, decision, and audit record together

source_timestamp.description: when the underlying observation was taken (epoch seconds)

inference_timestamp.description: when the model produced this output (epoch seconds)

input_quality.description: quality of the data the model consumed

output_quality.description: quality the model claims for its output

value_kind.description: measured, derived, or predicted — a prediction must never read as a measurement

feature_pipeline_version.description: the feature pipeline is part of the model's identity

calibration_version.description: which calibration the value rests on

valid_until.description: freshness bound (epoch seconds) — past this, the proposal is stale by construction

sync_state.description: the twin's synchronization state at inference time

service_health.description: health of the service producing the payload

fallback_state.description: what the plant falls back to if this is rejected

requested_authority.description: authority level 0-5 the proposal asks for — the gate's primary input

action_type.description: what the proposal would do if accepted

uncertainty.description: uncertainty on the value, in its own units

confidence.description: model confidence, where the family defines one

prediction_horizon_s.description: how far ahead a prediction reaches

acknowledged.description: whether the gate accepted the proposal

rejection_reason.description: why the gate rejected it, if it did

audit_identity.description: who or what committed the decision

$comment: GENERATED EXAMPLE — demonstrates the format produced by the cst toolkit from the example I/O list. Adapt to your project and review before use.
